---
title: "Physical Geography"
subtitle: "Fundamentals of the Earth's Physical Systems"
author: "Geography Team"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

{{< include physical-geography/00-syllabus.md >}}

---

Detailed coverage of this syllabus is divided into specialized modules. Please navigate to the following core subject areas for in-depth topic notes, diagrams, and video lectures:

### Unit I: Geomorphology
Focuses on the interior of the earth, plate tectonics, weathering, mass wasting, and cycles of erosion (Davis and Penck).
&rarr; **[Go to the Geomorphology Module](geomorphology.html)**

### Unit II: Climatology
Covers atmospheric structure, insolation, heat budget, winds, air masses, cyclones, and the Indian Monsoon mechanism.
&rarr; **[Go to the Climatology Module](climatology.html)**

### Unit III: Oceanography & Hydrology
Explores the hydrological cycle, ocean bottom topography, temperature, salinity, ocean deposits, and currents.
&rarr; **[Go to the Oceanography Module](oceanography.html)**


*(Note: Practical topics like topographic map interpretation and weather instruments are often covered during laboratory sessions. Please refer to your course instructor or the designated Practical Worksheets for Unit IV).*
